name: Beanstalk Governance Flash Loan Attack
tldr: Attacker flash-borrowed ~$1B in stablecoins from Aave/Uniswap/SushiSwap to acquire 79% of Beanstalk's Stalk voting power in a single block, then called emergencyCommit on a pre-planted malicious BIP that transferred all protocol assets (~$182M) to the attacker contract.
coreContradiction: Beanstalk's governance counted flash-borrowed liquidity-pool deposits as legitimate long-term voting power, so temporary capital worth $1B could irrevocably commit a protocol-draining proposal in the same transaction.

attackSteps.label: Plant malicious BIPs
attackSteps.detail: A day before the exploit, the attacker created two governance proposals: BIP-18 (drain all funds to attacker) and BIP-19 (donate $250k BEAN to Ukraine as distraction).

attackSteps.label: Flash-borrow ~$1B
attackSteps.detail: In a single transaction the attacker borrowed 350M DAI, 500M USDC, and 150M USDT from Aave, 32M BEAN from Uniswap V2, and 11.6M LUSD from SushiSwap.

attackSteps.label: Convert to BEAN3CRV-f LP tokens
attackSteps.detail: Deposited stablecoins into Curve 3pool for 3Crv, then deposited 3Crv into the BEAN3CRV-f Curve pool, obtaining BEAN3CRV-f LP tokens.

attackSteps.label: Deposit into Beanstalk (acquire Stalk)
attackSteps.detail: Deposited all BEAN3CRV-f tokens into the Beanstalk Diamond, which automatically minted Stalk voting power via LibSilo.incrementBipRoots, giving the attacker ~79% of total Stalk.

attackSteps.label: emergencyCommit BIP-18
attackSteps.detail: Called GovernanceFacet.emergencyCommit(18), which required only ≥67% Stalk approval; the malicious BIP used delegatecall to transfer all BEAN3CRV-f held by the Beanstalk Diamond to the attacker contract.

attackSteps.label: Unwind and repay flashloans
attackSteps.detail: Converted BEAN3CRV-f back through Curve into stablecoins, repaid all flash loans with fees, and retained ~24,830 ETH (~$76M) which was bridged to Tornado Cash.

fundFlow.note: All protocol funds transferred via emergencyCommit delegatecall in BIP-18
After repaying $1B flashloans; proceeds sent to Tornado Cash
